[2019-01-22 22:51] winged: Eventually someone mentioned it offhand in a different discord server and mentioned it being free to play. And I was like "oh shit that's still a thing?" and it totally is a thing! I downloaded it on Steam and went straight to the campaign and had a blast
[2019-01-22 22:51] winged: Prismata does a ton of things super well to get players into the game (at least, to get me into the game):
[2019-01-22 22:52] winged: 0) The game is fundamentally just kinda really good. Like, it shows in all the moments you actually play.
[2019-01-22 22:53] winged: 1) The campaign is really kinda delightful? I found the characters likeable and the art kinda cute. Like, I'd remembered the art from way way back in the original days and I was surprised that the game devs had managed to take all those units and diegetically insert them into the game (like the tarsiers). The game is both kinda neat in a colourful-future-world sense and also in a slapstick-dnd-humor sense
[2019-01-22 22:53] winged: the whole thing feels very much like a tabletop RPG party dicking around
[2019-01-22 22:54] winged: hyper competent exaggerated cocksure dorks being thrown from one contrived combat circumstance into the next, wisecracking all the day. +1
[2019-01-22 22:54] winged: 2) Outside the combat, there's also the Quick Guides section which is like... really really straightforward and uses neat pictograms for things. I'm gonna leave a picture and come back to it later:
[2019-01-22 22:55] winged: Like, it's great that I can just go to the internal documentation for how to absorb stuff and there's neat diagramming
[2019-01-22 22:56] winged: 3) The game also has a ton of singleplayer puzzles / challenges. I kinda poked at them in between campaign missions, and did 5 of the first few sets later on the Path to Glory
[2019-01-22 22:56] winged: 4) the Path To Glory is great. There's always something to do and it's right there in front of me
[2019-01-22 22:57] winged: 5) The wiki is also great: a lot of game elements point directly to the wiki (it's like, besides every unit in some section of the game UI?) and the wiki goes into serious detail on the strategies of every little unit. I lost hard to a thorium dynamo strategy once and I could just go to the wiki and read up on the thing
[2019-01-22 22:59] winged: 6) Finally, this is subtle, but the Watch tab did a lot to make me feel excited to play the game. It always displays the topmost live game, which both:
- implies that players are actually playing this game
- lets me dive in and see that the game looks really cool at high levels of play! I constantly jump in to games to watch and see like 7 different classes of unit being bought on both sides and players taking a long time to think through their turns.
[2019-01-22 23:00] winged: It really helps build confidence that the game is alive, vibrant, diverse, tense.... just healthy? even at a high level? With a deterministic open information game my immediate assumptions are that the game would get kinda degenerative at high level play cause high level Magic and the like is all about being consistent and this is a game where you can totally just spam 1 unit all game long (supply limits withstanding)
[2019-01-22 23:01] winged: Great game, very exciting to get into, but now I wanna focus on the 'bad stuff', which is mostly a single oddly shaped hole in how the game has been sold to me

[2019-01-22 23:01] winged: Missing Thing:

[2019-01-22 23:04] winged: The campaign didn't really do anything to disabuse me of the notion: I'd get a totally unique set of units and my opponent would have their own set of units and that was that

messages.content: I'd hit the Quick Play button

messages.content:

Prismata in 60 Seconds

messages.author: winged

messages.author: winged

messages.author: winged

yeah it's why I thought... uh... let me find this:
```
so then I thought (and told my friends even!) that players customize their own decks I think, and then the game like... selects a random set of units from both player's customized decks and then set up the match?
```

messages.author: winged
messages.content: I kinda thought this for an embarrassingly long amount of time

messages.author: winged
messages.content: like, here's a question I have as a newbie: first player starts with N drones and the second players starts with N+1 drones? I'm not sure what the numbers are. 6/7? 7/8?

messages.author: shadourow
messages.content: err... Ok, I guess I'll just answer : what the point ?

messages.author: winged
messages.content: like, there's nowhere saying "Prismata is about killing all your opponent's units. Players start with 6 and 7 drones, and then take turns building units from the supply: the supply is a symmetric combination of the Standard Set plus a Random Set chosen from all units in the game."

messages.author: shadourow
messages.content: Like, you can see it in an actual game

messages.author: shadourow
messages.content: why should it be written somewhere ?

messages.author: shadourow
messages.content: is the 6 and 7 drone count important to understand the game ?

messages.author: winged
messages.content:
because 1) I think it's kind of a shame that so much effort has gone into quadruply documenting how the rest of the game mechanics works while this hasn't been mentioned anywhere and
2) Knowing how games were set up explicitly in advance would've really helped me get into the game faster and get my friends into the game faster

messages.author: winged
messages.content: I mention the 6/7 thing not because it's important

messages.author: winged
messages.content: but because it kinda spells out the whole "the opening is never actually documented anywhere" gap I found

messages.author: winged
messages.content:
like, I even found this page: https://prismata.gamepedia.com/Rules
Rules

messages.author: winged
messages.content: it has a section called 'Comprehensive Rules' and it doesn't explain the random set or the 6/7

messages.author: shadourow
messages.content: Erf... you need to realize that the wiki is made by players, for players, when a player usually discover the wiki, he usually know the basic rules of the game

messages.author: awaclus
messages.content: I suppose the fact that it's Dominion is something that everyone pretty much takes for granted (no pun intended) at this point, so failing to communicate that is an easy oversight to make

messages.author: shadourow
messages.content: There never was a point for a player to make a page about the basic rules of the game

messages.author: winged
messages.content: I'm not blaming you guys, I'm just trying to point to a hole here that I really stumbled upon. The game is incredibly fun to play and has an absolute truck load of learning tools
